concerns.text: The plan cites 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/klzhong/klzhong/ligo/resolve/main/data/train-00000-of-00001.parquet' as the source for LIGO O3 noise PSD. This URL points to a specific training parquet file (likely for a machine learning model) rather than the standard LIGO Open Science Center (LOSC) O3 PSD data required for PyCBC injection. PyCBC expects a PSD object or a time-series strain file with specific metadata, not a generic ML training parquet. Using a training dataset as a noise source for physical injection risks mismatched sampling rates, frequency resolution, or statistical properties, rendering the 'instrumental noise floor' baseline invalid for the quantization study.

The plan proposes a fallback to 'generating a theoretical PSD using pycbc.psd' if the HuggingFace source fails. While theoretically sound, the primary reliance on a non-standard ML training dataset (klzhong/ligo) for the 'Verified Datasets' requirement is inappropriate. The study requires a verified, standard O3 PSD (e.g., from GWOSC or the official LIGO data releases) to ensure the 'instrumental error' baseline is physically accurate. Relying on a third-party ML training set introduces an unverified variable into the core noise model.
